summary.en: A rarely listed clause type: swap a single-word object complement for a whole clause. It pairs with complex transitive verbs like call, make, and consider.
summary.zh: 一种很多体系都不收录的从句：把单个词的宾语补语换成一整个从句。它对应 call、make、consider 这类复杂及物动词。

heading.en: Which verbs need an object complement
heading.zh: 哪些动词需要宾语补语

body.en: Among the five verb classes, the complex transitive (Pattern 4) verb takes an object but still feels incomplete — it needs an object complement that adds info about that object. Verbs like call, make, and consider work this way: 'You can call me Papa Rabbit' (me = object, Papa Rabbit = object complement).
body.zh: 在五大类动词中，复杂及物动词（句型四）带一个宾语却仍不完整——它需要一个宾语补语来补充关于宾语的信息。call、make、consider 就是这样：“You can call me Papa Rabbit”（me 是宾语，Papa Rabbit 是宾语补语）。

concepts.sourceRef: 复杂及物动词 call 的宾语

heading.en: Replace the complement with a clause
heading.zh: 把宾语补语换成一个从句

body.en: Take the single word that fills the object complement slot and swap in a whole clause instead. The clause now does the job of the object complement — that is an object complement clause. 'Papa Rabbit' becomes 'what you like': You can call me what you like.
body.zh: 把充当宾语补语的那个词，替换成一整个从句，这个从句就充当了宾语补语——这就是宾语补语从句。把“Papa Rabbit”换成“what you like”：You can call me what you like。

heading.en: Noun-like or adjective-like — classification is just a label
heading.zh: 可名可形——分类只是个标签

body.en: We file object complement clauses under noun clauses, but they needn't behave like a noun: 'who I am today' simply replaces the adjective 'smart' in 'made me smart', so it can be adjective-like too. Because the type is uncommon, many systems skip it entirely — don't be surprised if you never see it named. What matters is the principle, not the label.
body.zh: 我们把宾语补语从句归在名词从句里，但它不一定有名词性质：“who I am today”不过是把“made me smart”里的形容词“smart”替换掉，所以也可能是形容词性质。因为这类从句少见，很多体系干脆略过它——别的分类里没有它也别吃惊。重要的是背后的原理，而不是这个标签。

examples.text: You can call me Papa Rabbit.

note.en: Baseline simple sentence: 'me' is the object of complex transitive 'call'; 'Papa Rabbit' is the object complement that fills in 'me'.
note.zh: 基础简单句：“me”是复杂及物动词“call”的宾语，“Papa Rabbit”是补充“me”的宾语补语。

examples.text: You can call me what you like.

note.en: Swap the proper noun 'Papa Rabbit' for the clause 'what you like' — now a clause does the object complement's job. Meaning: call me whatever you want.
note.zh: 把专有名词“Papa Rabbit”换成从句“what you like”——从句充当了宾语补语。意思是：你可以随便叫我什么。

examples.text: My education made me who I am today.

note.en: 'Who I am today' is the object complement clause — it just replaces the adjective 'smart' in 'made me smart'. So an object complement clause can be adjective-like, not only noun-like.
note.zh: “who I am today”是宾语补语从句——它只是替换了“made me smart”里的形容词“smart”。所以宾语补语从句可以是形容词性质，不只是名词性质。

prompt.en: An object complement clause pairs with which verb class?
prompt.zh: 宾语补语从句对应哪一类动词？

explain.en: Object complement clauses correspond to the complex transitive (Pattern 4) verbs — the ones that take an object plus an object complement.
explain.zh: 宾语补语从句对应复杂及物动词（句型四）——那种既带宾语又带宾语补语的动词。

prompt.en: Complete the clause so it means 'call me whatever you want': You can call me ____ you like.
prompt.zh: 补全从句，使其表示“随便叫我什么”：You can call me ____ you like。

explain.en: 'What you like' is the object complement clause that replaces the proper noun 'Papa Rabbit'.
explain.zh: “what you like”是替换专有名词“Papa Rabbit”的宾语补语从句。

prompt.en: In 'My education made me who I am today', the clause 'who I am today' replaces what kind of word?
prompt.zh: 在“My education made me who I am today”中，从句“who I am today”替换了什么样的词？

explain.en: It stands in for the adjective 'smart' — which is why an object complement clause can be adjective-like, not only noun-like.
explain.zh: 它替换了形容词“smart”——所以宾语补语从句可以是形容词性质，不只是名词性质。

quizzes.sentence: You can call me what do you like.
quizzes.fix: You can call me what you like.

explain.en: An embedded object complement clause uses statement word order, not question order — drop the auxiliary 'do': 'what you like'.
explain.zh: 内嵌的宾语补语从句用陈述语序，而非疑问语序——去掉助动词“do”：“what you like”。

title.en: From one word to a whole clause
title.zh: 从一个词到一整句从句

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: You can call me Big Bad Wolf.

lines.speaker: Rabbit
lines.text: Fine — you can call me what you like.

note.en: Same slot, now a clause: an object complement clause.
note.zh: 同一个位置，换成从句：宾语补语从句。

lines.speaker: Wolf
lines.text: Hard work made me who I am today.

note.en: Here the clause replaces an adjective — adjective-like.
note.zh: 这里从句替换了一个形容词——形容词性质。
